commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds a `captionedFinalText` channel capability and changes ACP final-mode TTS to suppress live Telegram block text, deliver one captioned voice note, and cover the flow with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +95, Tests +415, Generated 0. Total +510 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main settles ACP visible text before final TTS, and the inspected Telegram Desktop proof shows the baseline separate text-plus-voice behavior versus the candidate single captioned voice message.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix final-failure accounting and add focused regression coverage without a product decision.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" on **ACP-dispatched turns** — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened because `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` settled visible text **before** `maybeApplyTtsToPayload()`, so block text was always flushed to the channel before audio synthesis even started.

This PR eliminates the ACP-path churn by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability and suppressing live ACP block delivery when captioned final TTS will be used.

**Scope**: This PR fixes the ACP dispatch path only, which is the primary production path for Telegram TTS. The non-ACP generic dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`) has a similar text-before-voice pattern but is pre-existing behavior and out of scope for this PR — it should be addressed in a follow-up issue.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. ACP projector delivers block text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. suppressBlockUserDelivery = true → NO live block text delivered to user
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement for captioned final TTS; exclude `tagged` auto-mode from suppression (tagged mode is directive-conditional, unknowable at block time) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | Delivery coordinator: `suppressBlockUserDelivery` gate returns false for block delivery; accumulates text for fallback |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 3 regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression + fallback, tagged-mode streaming preserved, tagged+directive TTS supplement |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tagged auto-TTS mode fix

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. The suppression gate now correctly excludes `tagged` mode from `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`, preserving live block streaming for ordinary replies. Tagged replies with directives still get TTS audio delivered as a supplement with `visibleTextAlreadyDelivered: true`.

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 61 passed (61)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 73 tests passed across 3 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode "delete-and-resend churn" on ACP-dispatched turns — when TTS mode is `final`, users saw a text message appear, then a separate voice note arrived afterward. Root cause: ACP `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` settled visible text before TTS synthesis.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `e33c474a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`, `messages.tts.auto: "always"`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "block"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch via `npm link`
  2. Restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Sent messages from Telegram to the bot
  4. Observed: voice note with caption appeared directly — no text flash

- **Evidence after fix (live Telegram screenshot)**:

  ![After fix — voice messages with caption, no text churn](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/6937B66CC8A27C33B76FBD54155B542C)

  Voice notes delivered as single message with text caption. No separate text messages created.

- **What was not tested**: Non-ACP dispatch path (out of scope, pre-existing behavior). Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only).

## Out of scope / Follow-up

The non-ACP generic dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`) also has a text-before-voice pattern for final-mode TTS: blocks stream live, then TTS audio arrives as a separate supplement. This is pre-existing behavior unrelated to the regression reported in #83511 (which specifically describes ACP `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` ordering). A follow-up issue should be filed to address non-ACP TTS churn if desired.

Closes #83511
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
